tldr: Three of your five ads are past their expiry date, and two more are close behind. The bigger issue: every single ad is the same swipeable format with the same emoji-heavy sustainability pitch, so Meta treats them as one ad and quietly shows just one.

benchmark.context: Typical brands in natural bedding put out a fresh ad every three weeks, and the best ones do it every two. You're running just five ads and they all look and sound alike, so Meta can't tell them apart and ends up showing fewer of them to fewer people.

ads.headline: 🐑 Wool Wadding with Natural Loft & Strength 💛
ads.body: All-purpose natural wadding for quilting, upholstery, padding & more - combining soft comfort with strong & lasting support 🌿 Crafted from wool, it naturally wicks moisture, offers fire-resistant properties, and is a sustainable choice designed to last over time 🔥🌾
drivers: This ad has been running unchanged for 87 days, more than three times your normal refresh window.
The headline stacks three emojis around a product description with no clear benefit for the buyer.
The body tries to sell to quilters, upholsterers and DIY-ers in one paragraph, so no single shopper feels it was written for them.

ads.headline: Upgrade What You Sleep On 🛌🌿✨
ads.body: Whether you prefer soft or firm, low or high support, our range is designed to suit every sleep style, helping you stay aligned and comfortable throughout the night 🌙💫 Australian-made using microplastic-free, sustainable, and compostable materials 🌿💚
drivers: This one has also been live 87 days without a single change, well past when shoppers start scrolling past it.
The opening line 'Upgrade What You Sleep On' could describe any bedding brand on the planet, so nothing pulls the eye.
The claim list (Australian-made, microplastic-free, compostable, sustainable) is the same one you already made in three other ads, so it stops registering.

ads.headline: 60/40 Wool/Poly Wadding
drivers: The body copy is one line long and just names three product types, so the shopper has no reason to click.
The headline is a product spec, not a hook, and won't stop anyone mid-scroll.
It has been running 43 days, past your normal 28-day mark, and the thin copy makes fatigue arrive faster.

ads.headline: 🌿 Wool Weed Matting That Works Naturally 💚
ads.body: Take control of your soil with weed matting that reduces unwanted plant growth, improves moisture retention, and naturally enhances soil quality for long-term performance 🌿
drivers: The leaf-emoji-then-headline-then-heart-emoji pattern is copied from three of your other live ads, which is exactly what makes Meta treat them all as one.
42 days in, and it's selling to gardeners while every other ad targets sleepers, so no audience gets a consistent story from you.
The copy lists three benefits back-to-back with no proof, no customer voice and no visual of the matting in real soil.

ads.headline: ✨Your Perfect Doona Awaits 🛌
ads.body: Experience a deeper, calmer, and more breathable sleep with organic wool bedding that naturally regulates temperature, feels incredibly soft, and supports your body night after night 🌿😴✨ Proudly Australian-made, our bedding is completely microplastic-free, offering a cleaner & more conscious way to rest while choosing a product that's both sustainable and fully compostable at the end of its long use💚
drivers: The body is 65 words of feature-listing with no single moment a shopper can picture themselves in.
'Australian-made, microplastic-free, sustainable, compostable' is the exact same phrase block used in your pillow ad, which is why Meta lumps them together.
Only 29 days old but already showing the same fatigue shape as your older ads because it repeats their words and their look.

hero_concept.hook: The one bedding layer nobody talks about.
hero_concept.headline: Sleep on wool, not plastic.
hero_concept.primary_text:
Most mattress toppers are polyurethane foam wrapped in polyester. You breathe that in for a third of your life.

Our coir and natural rubber sheets are the layer underneath your sheet: springy coconut fibre bonded with tapped rubber, breathable, firm, and completely free of microplastics. Made in Australia, compostable at the end of a very long life.

Swap the foam. Feel the difference on night one.
hero_concept.visual_direction: A clean overhead shot of the coir and rubber sheet laid flat on a raw timber floor, with a folded linen bedsheet placed on one corner to show scale and context.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Every one of your live ads leads with a leafy headline and an emoji parade, so they blur together and shoppers scroll past. This ad opens with a real problem (foam and plastic in bedding) and shows one product doing one job, which is the kind of ad your account is missing.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a clean overhead flat-lay on a raw pale timber floor with a single folded natural linen bedsheet placed at one corner for scale, with soft diffused daylight from a large window casting gentle directional shadows. Calm, natural, editorial-catalogue aesthetic with a muted earthy palette and generous negative space. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL - preserve the exact coir fibre and natural rubber material appearance: the dense woven coconut-husk fibre texture in its natural warm coconut-brown colour bonded to a matte natural latex rubber backing, strictly NO colour shift to grey, black, orange, red-brown, or synthetic-looking finish, NO glossy or plastic sheen, NO change to foam or synthetic material appearance. STRUCTURE - preserve the exact construction: rectangular flat sheet form with visible coir fibre weave texture on the top surface and matte rubber layer visible along the cut edge, same thickness profile and same edge finish as shown in the reference. Do NOT add stitching, binding tape, labels, printed graphics, or edging that is not in the reference, do NOT change the rectangular shape, do NOT alter the thickness, do NOT convert it to a foam or textile mat, do NOT add decorative patterns to the surface. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself - text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: Two of your ads stop working inside a week, and two more inside about two and a half weeks.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Wool Valley refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Colita, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep - work with us or don't.

method_note: Based only on your public Facebook and Instagram ads: the pictures, the words, and how long each has been running. No access to your account or private numbers.
